meta.title: Leading & Lagging KPI Framework — {organisation / function / site}
meta.subtitle: Balanced HSE performance indicators · ISO 45001:2018 clause 9.1 · each indicator defined · anti-gaming safeguards
meta.author: {performance / assurance owner role}
meta.deid_notice: This KPI framework reports AGGREGATE performance indicators only — never an individual's safety record. No metric is attributed to a nameable person; any per-person or per-team breakdown of fewer than 5 is suppressed (secondary suppression applied) so it cannot be back-calculated; indicator owners are role labels and no re-identification key appears. Lagging rates are computed to standard definitions, never invented. Decision-support; a competent person must review this framework before adoption.

sections.text: A balanced leading/lagging HSE KPI framework for the {organisation / function / site}, designed against ISO 45001:2018 clause 9.1 (monitoring, measurement, analysis & performance evaluation). The set pairs reactive/outcome (lagging) indicators with active/predictive (leading) indicators — a lagging-only scorecard is rejected as a reactive-only picture. Every indicator is fully defined and every countable target carries an anti-gaming safeguard.

This framework DESIGNS / normalises the indicator set. It does not compute a given injury rate (see incident-rate-calculator) or own the API RP 754 process-safety tiers (process-safety-kpi) — those are referenced as domain exemplars. Lagging rates are computed by the incident_rates engine to standard definitions (anchors TRIR 2.07 / LTIFR 6.00), never invented.

sections.caption: Each leading indicator carries formula · source · frequency · owner · target — a bare indicator with no definition fails specificity (resolved from KB-DATA-LEADING-INDICATORS).

rows: incident_rates standard definition (TRIR anchor 2.07 / LTIFR anchor 6.00)
sections.caption: Lagging RATES are computed by incident_rates, never invented; a missing work-hours denominator is [GAP]. A lagging-only set fails the balance gate — it is paired with the leading indicators above.

metrics.label: Leading indicators
metrics.caption: active / predictive half of the balance

metrics.label: Culture maturity
metrics.caption: mature shifts the mix toward leading

sections.text: The leading/lagging mix is matched to culture maturity per KB-SNIP-KPI-DESIGN: reactive → developing → mature shifts the balance toward leading. A set with no leading half is flagged as a clause-9.1 imbalance and rebalanced before adoption.

rows.title: {countable target at risk of gaming — e.g. raw incident count}
rows.description: incentivises under-reporting — paired with a quality/assurance safeguard ({audited reporting-quality / near-miss reporting-rate cross-check})

sections.text: Any countable target is stress-tested for a perverse incentive and paired with a quality/assurance safeguard. A gameable metric with no safeguard fails the defensibility gate and is not presented.
Road-safety indicators (ISO 39001:2012) — road / transport / fleet scope only

rows: (journeys within limit ÷ total) × 100; or over-speed events ÷ distance
(pre-use checks with a defect ÷ checks done) × 100
ISO 39001:2012 fatigue factor (computation cross-refs SUB-03 / LOG-01, not here)
sections.caption: The LEAD-06 road-safety EXTEND branch (D-01) — the single home of road-safety KPIs (one skill). Built from KB-DATA-ROAD-SAFETY-INDICATORS only when the scope is road / transport / fleet; + seatbelt / helmet use where occupants / two-wheelers are in scope. LEAD-06 owns the indicator; the engine owns the computation.

rows.control: {control implied by a leading indicator — e.g. engineering fix for a recurring defect trend}

sections.text: Where a leading indicator implies a control action, KB-SNIP-HOC is applied — a measure ranks above admin/PPE; a PPE-only treatment is never the sole response.

define every indicator (formula·source·frequency·owner·target) → compute lagging rates via incident_rates → review the balance and the targets against maturity {cadence} → analyse performance per ISO 45001:2018 clause 9.1 → feed evaluated performance into management review. The framework maps to clause 9.1 via KB-SNIP-LEADERSHIP-CLAUSE-MAP (this skill is the primary owner). Decision-support; a competent person must review before adoption.
